seoTitle: Deddington vs Deep Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Deddington and Deep Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Deddington vs Deep Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Deddington and Deep Bay.
heading: Deddington vs Deep Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Deddington, Tasmania and Deep Bay, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Deddington has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Deep Bay (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Deddington or Deep Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Deddington has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Deep Bay scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Deddington scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Deep Bay scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Deddington or Deep Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Deddington has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.88% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
